summary: Matched the Sheik vanish item implementation by replacing asm/melee/it/items/itseakvanish.s with a new C source and header. The PR moved itseakvanish into the C build, defined its item state table, spawn/setup/update/destroy-style wrappers, and cleaned up local extern declarations by exporting shared item helper prototypes through headers.
what_changed: Added src/melee/it/items/itseakvanish.c with matched C implementations for it_802B1C60, it_802B1D38, it_802B1D40, it_802B1DAC, and it_802B1DCC.
Added the item state table it_803F70B8 in C with one entry using it_802B1DAC as the callback and NULL for the remaining two callback slots.
Changed obj_files.mk so itseakvanish builds from src/melee/it/items/itseakvanish.c.o instead of asm/melee/it/items/itseakvanish.s.o.
Added src/melee/it/items/itseakvanish.h declaring it_803F70B8 and the public itseakvanish functions.
Updated src/melee/it/it_803F3100.c to include <melee/it/items/itseakvanish.h> and remove local extern declarations for it_802B1D38, it_802B1DCC, and it_803F70B8.
Added shared prototypes for it_8027518C and it_802751D8 to src/melee/it/it_266F.h and removed a duplicate local declaration of it_802751D8 from itcapsule.c.
smart_moves: Used the SpawnItem struct directly in it_802B1C60 to mirror the assembly stack setup: kind 0x55, prev_pos copied from input, pos copied from prev_pos, zero velocity, zero damage, parent fields set from the owner GObj, and spawn.x44_flag.bits.b0 enabled.
Preserved the null-check behavior after Item_80268B18: only call it_802B1D40 and db_80225DD8 when item creation succeeds, otherwise return NULL.
Encoded assembly constants as meaningful C assignments where available, including item->xD44_lifeTimer = 60.0F and Item_80268E5C(..., ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE).
Moved declarations into headers instead of leaving ad hoc local externs in consumer files, making later item decompilation less dependent on duplicated prototypes.
Kept simple wrapper functions simple: it_802B1DAC returns it_802751D8(item_gobj), and it_802B1DCC forwards to it_8026B894(item_gobj, gobj).
decomp_lessons: For small item object files, matching often consists of translating SpawnItem stack initialization faithfully and then routing lifecycle callbacks through existing item helper functions.
When replacing an asm item with C, update obj_files.mk at the same time so the build switches from the assembly object to the C object.

If a newly decompiled item owns an ItemStateTable used by central item tables, expose that table from the item-specific header and include it at the central use site instead of keeping a local extern.
Shared item helper prototypes such as it_8027518C and it_802751D8 should be promoted to common item headers when multiple item source files need them.
Assembly constants can become clearer C when tied to existing fields and macros, such as xD44_lifeTimer, xDAC_itcmd_var0, owner, and ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE.
naming_conventions: New item-specific files follow the item module naming pattern: src/melee/it/items/itseakvanish.c and src/melee/it/items/itseakvanish.h.
The header guard uses the project style MELEE_IT_ITSEAKVANISH_H.
Unidentified functions retain address-based names such as it_802B1C60 and it_802B1D40.
The state table retains its address-based data symbol name it_803F70B8.
Function parameters in the C implementation use descriptive names where evidence is clear: parent, pos, dir, item_gobj, and owner.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Translated the assembly's stack-based SpawnItem construction into a local SpawnItem spawn variable with ordered field assignments.
Matched the assembly's vector copy pattern by assigning spawn.prev_pos = *pos and then spawn.pos = spawn.prev_pos.
Matched zero float initialization using chained velocity assignments: spawn.vel.x = spawn.vel.y = spawn.vel.z = 0.0F.
Matched the r0/r5 bit insertion into the spawn flags with spawn.x44_flag.bits.b0 = 1.
Represented the assembly's 0x42700000 sdata2 float as 60.0F for item->xD44_lifeTimer.
Replaced trivial assembly functions with direct C returns/calls: return false, return it_802751D8(item_gobj), and it_8026B894(item_gobj, gobj).
follow_up_queries: Find other item PRs that replace asm/melee/it/items/*.s with C and update obj_files.mk.
Search for remaining local extern declarations of it_8027518C or it_802751D8 that should use it_266F.h.
Search for SpawnItem initialization patterns using kind 0x55 and x44_flag.bits.b0.
Compare other item state table migrations that add item-specific headers and remove externs from it_803F3100.c.
